2606.32027.title: Freeform Preference Learning for Robotic Manipulation
2606.32027.paper_summary: Reward design remains a central bottleneck for autonomous robot policy improvement, especially in long-horizon manipulation tasks where sparse success labels provide too little signal and binary preferences collapse many competing notions of quality into one ambiguous signal. We introduce Freeform Preference Learning (FPL), a method for learning robot policies from freeform human preferences. Rather than asking annotators which of two trajectories is better overall, FPL lets them define natural-language preference axes, such as speed, safety, quality of placement, or carefulness, and provide pairwise preferences along each axis. These annotations are used to learn a language-conditioned reward model that maps a trajectory and preference label to an axis-specific reward. We use this model to train a reward-conditioned policy that optimizes across the multiple human-specified dimensions. Across four real-world and two simulated long-horizon manipulation tasks, FPL improves over sparse-reward and binary-preference methods by 38 percentage points. Beyond improved performance, FPL learns dense progress signals without explicit subtask segmentation, shows compositionality of behavior not present in the data, and allows users to steer the policy towards different behaviors at test time without retraining. Blog post with videos available at https://freeform-pl.github.io/fpl.website/
2606.32027.paper_summary_zh: 獎勵設計仍然是自主機器人政策改進的主要瓶頸，特別是在長期操作任務中，稀疏的成功標籤提供的信號過少，而二元偏好將許多競爭的質量概念壓縮成一個模糊的信號。我們介紹了自由形式偏好學習（Freeform Preference Learning, FPL），這是一種從自由形式人類偏好中學習機器人政策的方法。FPL 讓標註者定義自然語言的偏好軸，例如速度、安全性、放置質量或小心程度，而不是詢問他們哪兩條軌跡整體上更好，並在每個軸上提供成對的偏好。這些註釋用於學習一個語言條件獎勵模型，該模型將一條軌跡和偏好標籤映射到特定軸的獎勵。我們使用這個模型來訓練一個獎勵條件政策，該政策在多個人類指定的維度上進行優化。在四個真實世界和兩個模擬的長期操作任務中，FPL 相較於稀疏獎勵和二元偏好方法提高了 38 個百分點。除了性能的提升，FPL 還在沒有明確子任務分割的情況下學習到密集的進展信號，顯示出數據中不存在的行為組合性，並允許用戶在測試時不需要重新訓練就能將政策引導向不同的行為。博客文章及視頻可在 https://freeform-pl.github.io/fpl.website/ 獲得。
2606.32027.author: Marcel Torne et.al.
2606.32027.authors: Marcel Torne, Anubha Mahajan, Abhijnya Bhat, Chelsea Finn
